ORDER BY s.Zip

records.question: Consider the average difference between K-12 enrollment and 15-17 enrollment of schools that are locally funded, list the names and DOC type of schools which has a difference above this average.
records.gold_sql: SELECT T2.School, T2.DOC FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.FundingType = 'Locally funded' AND (T1.`Enrollment (K-12)` - T1.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) > (SELECT AVG(T3.`Enrollment (K-12)` - T3.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) FROM frpm AS T3 INNER JOIN schools AS T4 ON T3.CDSCode = T4.CDSCode WHERE T4.FundingType = 'Locally funded')
records.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    s.School,
    s.DOCType
FROM schools s
JOIN frpm f ON s.CDSCode = f.CDSCode
WHERE (f.`Enrollment (K-12)` - f.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`) > (
    SELECT AVG(f2.`Enrollment (K-12)` - f2.`Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`)
    FROM frpm f2
    JOIN schools s2 ON f2.CDSCode = s2.CDSCode
    WHERE (s2.FundingType = 'Locally funded' OR f2.`Charter Funding Type` = 'Locally funded')
)
AND (s.FundingType = 'Locally funded' OR f.`Charter Funding Type` = 'Locally funded')

records.question: Of the schools that offers a magnet program serving a grade span of Kindergarten to 8th grade, how many offers Multiple Provision Types? records.pred_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT s.City) AS number_of_cities, COUNT(*) AS total_schools
FROM schools s
JOIN frpm f ON s.CDSCode = f.CDSCode
WHERE s.Magnet = 1
  AND f.`NSLP Provision Status` = 'Multiple Provision Types'
  AND s.GSoffered LIKE '%K%'
  AND s.GSoffered LIKE '%8%'
GROUP BY s.City

records.question: What is the annual average number of sets that were released between 1/1/2012 to 12/31/2015? Indicate the common langugage of the card.
records.gold_sql: SELECT (CAST(SUM(T1.id) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.id)) / 4, T2.language FROM sets AS T1 INNER JOIN set_translations AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.id WHERE T1.releaseDate BETWEEN '2012-01-01' AND '2015-12-31' GROUP BY T1.releaseDate ORDER BY COUNT(T2.language) DESC LIMIT 1
records.pred_sql:
-- Compute the annual average number of sets released between 2012 and 2015
-- and the most common language among those sets (using set_translations)
WITH yearly_counts AS (
    SELECT strftime('%Y', releaseDate) AS year, COUNT(*) AS cnt
    FROM sets
    WHERE releaseDate BETWEEN '2012-01-01' AND '2015-12-31'
    GROUP BY year
),
language_counts AS (
    SELECT st.language, COUNT(*) AS lang_cnt
    FROM sets s
    JOIN set_translations st ON s.code = st.setCode
    WHERE s.releaseDate BETWEEN '2012-01-01' AND '2015-12-31'
    GROUP BY st.language
)
SELECT 
    CAST(AVG(yc.cnt) AS REAL) AS annual_average_sets,
    (SELECT language FROM language_counts ORDER BY lang_cnt DESC LIMIT 1) AS common_language
FROM yearly_counts yc

records.question: Who has the highest average finishing rate between the highest and shortest football player?
records.gold_sql: SELECT A FROM ( SELECT AVG(finishing) result, 'Max' A FROM Player AS T1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id WHERE T1.height = ( SELECT MAX(height) FROM Player ) UNION SELECT AVG(finishing) result, 'Min' A FROM Player AS T1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS T2 ON T1.player_api_id = T2.player_api_id WHERE T1.height = ( SELECT MIN(height) FROM Player ) ) ORDER BY result DESC LIMIT 1
records.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(CASE WHEN p.height = (SELECT MAX(height) FROM Player) THEN pa.finishing END) > 
             AVG(CASE WHEN p.height = (SELECT MIN(height) FROM Player) THEN pa.finishing END)
        THEN 'Tallest'
        ELSE 'Shortest'
    END AS player_type,
    MAX(
        AVG(CASE WHEN p.height = (SELECT MAX(height) FROM Player) THEN pa.finishing END),
        AVG(CASE WHEN p.height = (SELECT MIN(height) FROM Player) THEN pa.finishing END)
    ) AS highest_avg_finishing
FROM Player p
JOIN Player_Attributes pa ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id
WHERE p.height = (SELECT MAX(height) FROM Player) 
   OR p.height = (SELECT MIN(height) FROM Player)

records.question: What is the difference in the annual average consumption of the customers with the least amount of consumption paid in CZK for 2013 between SME and LAM, LAM and KAM, and KAM and SME?
records.gold_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'SME', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.CustomerID) - CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'LAM', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.CustomerID) , CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'LAM', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.CustomerID) - CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'KAM', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.CustomerID) , CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'KAM', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.CustomerID) - CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'SME', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.CustomerID) FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T1.Currency = 'CZK' AND T2.Consumption = ( SELECT MIN(Consumption) FROM yearmonth ) AND T2.Date BETWEEN 201301 AND 201312
records.pred_sql:
-- Step 1: Find customers with the least consumption in each segment for 2013
-- Step 2: Compute annual average consumption per segment for those customers
-- Step 3: Compute differences between segments

WITH 
-- Filter customers who paid in CZK and have transactions in 2013
czk_customers AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT c.CustomerID, c.Segment
    FROM customers c
    WHERE c.Currency = 'CZK'
),
-- Get total consumption per customer for 2013
customer_annual_consumption AS (
    SELECT ym.CustomerID, c.Segment, SUM(ym.Consumption) AS total_consumption
    FROM yearmonth ym
    JOIN czk_customers c ON ym.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
    WHERE ym.Date >= '201301' AND ym.Date <= '201312'
    GROUP BY ym.CustomerID, c.Segment
),
-- Find the minimum total consumption per segment
min_consumption_per_segment AS (
    SELECT Segment, MIN(total_consumption) AS min_consumption
    FROM customer_annual_consumption
    GROUP BY Segment
),
-- Get customers with that minimum consumption (could be multiple)
lowest_consumption_customers AS (
    SELECT cac.CustomerID, cac.Segment, cac.total_consumption
    FROM customer_annual_consumption cac
    JOIN min_consumption_per_segment mps ON cac.Segment = mps.Segment AND cac.total_consumption = mps.min_consumption
),
-- Compute annual average consumption per segment for these customers
-- annual average = total consumption / number of customers in that segment
segment_annual_average AS (
    SELECT 
        Segment,
        SUM(total_consumption) / CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) AS annual_avg
    FROM lowest_consumption_customers
    GROUP BY Segment
),
-- Pivot the averages for difference calculation
sme_avg AS (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_annual_average WHERE Segment = 'SME'),
lam_avg AS (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_annual_average WHERE Segment = 'LAM'),
kam_avg AS (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_annual_average WHERE Segment = 'KAM')
-- Compute the three differences
SELECT 
    ROUND((SELECT annual_avg FROM sme_avg) - (SELECT annual_avg FROM lam_avg), 4) AS diff_SME_LAM,
    ROUND((SELECT annual_avg FROM lam_avg) - (SELECT annual_avg FROM kam_avg), 4) AS diff_LAM_KAM,
    ROUND((SELECT annual_avg FROM kam_avg) - (SELECT annual_avg FROM sme_avg), 4) AS diff_KAM_SME

records.question: For the customer who paid 634.8 in 2012/8/25, what was the consumption decrease rate from Year 2012 to 2013?
records.gold_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(SUBSTR(Date, 1, 4) = '2012', Consumption, 0)) - SUM(IIF(SUBSTR(Date, 1, 4) = '2013', Consumption, 0)) AS FLOAT) / SUM(IIF(SUBSTR(Date, 1, 4) = '2012', Consumption, 0)) FROM yearmonth WHERE CustomerID = ( SELECT T1.CustomerID FROM transactions_1k AS T1 INNER JOIN gasstations AS T2 ON T1.GasStationID = T2.GasStationID WHERE T1.Date = '2012-08-25' AND T1.Price = 634.8 )
records.pred_sql:
WITH target_customer AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT t.CustomerID
    FROM transactions_1k t
    WHERE t.Price = 634.8 AND t.Date = '2012-08-25'
),
consumption_by_year AS (
    SELECT 
        ym.CustomerID,
        SUBSTR(ym.Date, 1, 4) AS year,
        SUM(ym.Consumption) AS total_consumption
    FROM yearmonth ym
    JOIN target_customer tc ON ym.CustomerID = tc.CustomerID
    WHERE SUBSTR(ym.Date, 1, 4) IN ('2012', '2013')
    GROUP BY ym.CustomerID, SUBSTR(ym.Date, 1, 4)
)
SELECT 
    (CAST(MAX(CASE WHEN year = '2012' THEN total_consumption END) AS REAL) -
    CAST(MAX(CASE WHEN year = '2013' THEN total_consumption END) AS REAL)) / 
    CAST(MAX(CASE WHEN year = '2012' THEN total_consumption END) AS REAL) AS decrease_rate
FROM consumption_by_year
